title: Copy, Replace, Rename, Delete, Batch, and Concurrency · Execution Trace: Rename Nonatomic
summary: A rename is presented as atomic, replacement loses metadata, delete hides a live version, and concurrent writers silently overwrite each other. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the rename nonatomic increment in conditional object-mutation protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement rename as version-aware copy, validation, metadata update, reference switch, and safe source deletion with recoverable intermediate states.
Copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Rename Nonatomic · Theory Rename Nonatomic
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 9922 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement rename as version-aware copy, validation, metadata update, reference switch, and safe source deletion with recoverable intermediate states.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Rename Nonatomic contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Rename Nonatomic · Theory Rename Nonatomic.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Rename Nonatomic · Theory Rename Nonatomic, keep Rename Nonatomic connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.
